Insane class. Spend all her lecture talking about "what is assignment yes" yet still very vague in terms of grading, I honestly don't now how. Wasn't a SI major I literary don't know why I'm here.

Absolutely horrible teacher. Has no respect for her students and is not accommodating whatsoever. Lecture format is awful and she doesn't allow electronics. The homework is miserable. Not one good thing about her class and she hates her students

Gabi's approach killed my confidence and interest in UX research. I rarely understood what the instructional team expected from my work, as the rubrics were not thorough, and the team would not provide any additional feedback. Grading was extremely harsh, and points were deducted for things that were never described as issues prior to submission.

She's super moody and you NEED to attend class. There are no excuses for skipping, even in an urgent situation such as a family member dying (yes, I missed the first month of classes because of this). The graders are all over the place and the rubric is unclear. Overall the prof is horrid and the class format is even worse.

Not difficult to get an A, but that also depends on your GSI. The assignments are also graded by people they hire from outside of the school, and they're grading at times can conflict with what the GSIs say. Gabi really isn't that bad, but she does catch an attitude at times especially towards the end of the semester.

I think there may be some misattributed anger about the course that's directed at the professor. During lecture the she seemed more “cold,” but if you made it to office hours (which wasn't attended by more than 4 people), she was very helpful and patient. The design of the course though is horrid.

If you are not an SI major like me and are forced to take the class DO NOT TAKE IT. The class is a toss-up when it comes to grading because everything depends on who your grader that day is. Rubrics are way to broad, making it impossible to meet all the requirements. The professor is lost and doesn't even understand her own slides half the time.

Gabi was one of the most unclear, disorganized professors I have had. She was confusing with directions, assignments did not have clear rubrics or expectations, she would literally ask GSIs questions mid lecture because she did not know answers to questions. Her lectures and the worksheets were pointless, and everyone in the class was always lost.

Incredibly easy class-- requirements and expectations clearly defined for all assignments, relatively low time commitment. Gabi is curt but takes time in every lecture to address questions/concerns. GSIs/support staff seem to do a lot of the heavy lifting for assignments/overall course structure so kudos to them.

Assignments are due weekly on Saturday nights, grading criteria is not clear and not consistent. Essentially ensures that you will spend your Saturdays working on this class. She offers the ability to ask questions about assignment via slido in lecture and then slam dunks on confused students. Very rude and unprofessional.

Lecture content is mainly just repeated from slides and supplemented using YouTube videos but attendance is mandatory. Rubrics undergo the process of trial and error so the later assignments are more clear regarding grading criteria.

terrible professor but easy class. Yeah, she's nice, and a good person, but her lectures add absolutely no value whatsoever despite attendance being mandatory. Easy grading though. I breezed through with an A, but none of this is thanks to the professor's teaching ability.

I honestly do not get the reviews for Gabi at all. I really enjoyed having her as a professor. She was so helpful in office hours and over email, consistently took feedback on attendance policy and assignment drops/resubmissions, and actually made changes, and the class really is not overly difficult if you put in the time and read the rubric.

There's about 0 communication between her and the grading team. I found the lectures somewhat useful and engaging but she really does not emphasize important things to know for the assignments that we are being graded on. 4 for difficulty for unclear grading, 2 for the professor for the way this class is structured.

This was the worst SI class I've taken in the last two years. Attendance is ridiculous, doesn't allow more than 2 absences even if you have covid. Grading is insanely unclear and the professor is very rude.
Horrible professor. Amazing to me that she makes $130,000 a year. I have no idea how she got to be in this position of power because honestly she is a disgrace to the University of Michigan.

It seemed as if the instructor did not comprehend their own assignments. Sometimes in discussion when the instructor would attend, we would actually overhear the GSI of the discussion section having to explain the details of the assignment to the instructor, because the instructor did not know the details of their own assignments.

Happy to say I dropped out! Unless you want to make computer science or informational design your life, go for it, but the instructors go out of their way to make outsiders of the SI program feel left out. The GSI in charge is also extremely unhelpful; they grade papers as 15/100. Really awful, awful class and am grateful I got out in time.
Course policy is very confusing. It is hard to tell where you stand in the class, as well as what is expected of you. Despite COVID, she does not give excused absences.

most unclear and inconsistently graded course ever taken. students were extremely misled by the classes philosophy that you're not supposed to do every assignment - Marcu even ENCOURAGED us not to do every assignment. and when we did do every assignment, she literally changed the class so we couldn't. if u dont do everything there's no guaranteed A

Gabby is known for not knowing what she is doing and being a terrible lecturer. I will give her credits for being friendly, but that is not what students are paying 50K+ for. Her lectures are useless and she goes off topic. She assigns unreasonable amount of reading (100p+) per week and gives unreasonable assignments. Only took it bc it's required

I really enjoyed SI 422! Assignments were long but the rubric was so clear that it was really easy to do well on them if you take some time to write the reports. Content is really fascinating, but lectures were a little pointless. Gabi was also really great in office hours and very accommodating. Took our midterm feedback and made changes to course

The way she structures and runs her class makes no sense. The lectures are useless as she doesn't go over any of the important material ever, just leaves it up to the gsi's to do the heavy lifting. Also doesn't listen to feedback and gives an unreasonable amount work for a 3 credit class.

expected this class to be interesting and it has to do with my major but to be honest the way she runs the class is terrible. Its only 3 credits but she makes it a workload of a 5 credit class. Doesn't help the students learn the material and relies on the GSI's to do all the work, who aren't the best either. Doesn't respond to student suggestions.

Professor Marcu goes out of her way to make her students' lives harder. She is not professional in her lectures, makes everyone who asks questions feel stupid, and doesn't care about the success of her students. The class is a ton of busy-work but is easy. Not worth having to sit through her lectures, though.

He cannot teach. I've had bad math professors, but he is by far the worst. A lot of the content isn't that hard, but the way he teaches makes it seem so much harder. Also, I've learned some of this stuff before, but he still somehow made me feel confused about it. The GSIs are also completely unhelpful.

Genuinely, the worst professor I have ever come across. I have attended every lecture this semester, and this man has never been prepared to teach. For context, the most recent average of the course exam was a failing grade. I'm limited to 350 characters, so I can't share everything about this abomination of a course/professor. PLEASE RUN AWAY!

He's good at math but has absolutely no idea how to teach it. His lectures are erratic and confusing. The worksheets and problem sets don't match the midterms at all. Also, our practice midterms, which were last year's midterms, have been nothing like our actual midterms and were also much easier. Don't take this class if he is teaching it.

If I could describe in two words: "Academic Malpractice". Nothing says "great course" like chaos, failure, and a teaching team blaming students for their own incompetence. Lectures made no sense, homework felt randomized, and half the class bombed the midterm. This class is for math gods, not mortals. Avoid unless you hate yourself.

Try to avoid any classes with Grant. Hes passionate but simply doesn't know how to teach when it comes to people who are new to the subject matter. MSI is a program tailored for newer students to the field and the way he teaches is extremely erratic. He constantly provides wrong answers for in class problems and is utterly confusing. Good luck.

Math is already a tough subject for a lot of people and Schoenebeck does not make it easier. Clearly, he is very intelligent but does not know how to teach effectively. Very disorganized, does not provide enough guidance for problem-solving in class so homework is extremely challenging. Some HW/exam questions are extremely convoluted unnecessarily.

Terribly disorganized with many mistakes in materials that will lead you off course. Not accommodating if something comes up and you miss class or need an extension, in a way that conflicts with the syllabus. Homeworks and projects have lazy errors that take a lot of time to fix before doing real work. I've taken two courses and this is a pattern.

I recommend providing yourself an extra textbook with more examples and solved problems for verifying understanding along the way. Material isn't very hard, but there's a lot and it's nice to have a solid set of practice problems in addition to class materials.

this class has been nothing short of infuriating so far, which is shocking since it was already known to be so horrible as of last year, the first time it was offered. he can't properly or clearly convey many concepts and spends way too much class time in a disorganized state. exams have been fine so far, but most other things are so frustrating
The professor is fine.
Very arrogant and rude. Don't take 376 with him.

Lectures were pretty confusing and he spent too long having students do exercises and not enough time teaching the material. He lectured pretty far above the level of the class, meaning most students were lost.
The worst professor I have ever had. He knows nothing about how to teach a class. If you take his class, don't bother go to lectures, you are better on your own by just reading books.

He is so boring and a poor teacher. Wastes time going over concepts not on the exams. Refuses to: allow students to use laptops, post lecture slides, record lectures, or generally improve classes for students.

Schoenebeck is probably the best professor for this class. It's pretty hard material to communicate by spoken word; the textbook definitely contributed the most to my understanding. He writes pretty small, but is way more effective than the other professors at communicating examples. He really tries to help people to genuinely understand.

P. Shoenebeck was a great professor for 203. He's one of those profs that is really interested in his work and wants his students to know why. He knows where his students have trouble, and will talk a lot about how the intro topics relate to advanced research, which is how you successfully get students interested in the topic.
Not bad. The class is pretty theoretical but he tries to make sure everyone understands whats going on. Go for his office hours if you have any doubts

The course was such a mess. Doesn't understand what a reduction from 3 credit hours to 1.5 should look like (the workload is supposed to be halved). Jackie should take over next time or Hess should stop only teaching 1.5 credit hour courses. The format was frustratingly chaotic as you had no idea what to expect any given week. Cannot recommend
She is nice and always eager to help people and comes up to people and asks them if they need help. The class is very easy.
She was amazing! really caring and involved in our projects, and always there to help us out

The professor doesn't seem like she knows what she talks about. So late at grading things. Expects students to participate in 8am class while being so dry. She constantly preaches about accessibility and mental health but doesn't put it into action when students have a hard time ie. during finals week.

Students he doesn't see as already polished struggle to receive meaningful engagement. His comments about class, race, diversity are often reductive & create an environment that's alienating for students from lower income or marginalized backgrounds. Has a lot of industry experience—it's a shame that he can't be a better resource to all students.
Good lecturer!

Engaging at a lecturer at times but checked-out and seems like he has better things to do when you speak to him one-on-one. Also, my friend was taking an exam for another class when he busted into the room in the middle of it and demanded everyone to leave. Apparently he mistakenly thought he had the room booked. Caused a lot of panic.

He offers concrete industry skills, but his interactions with students are frequently rude, discouraging, and dismissive. I personally experienced and heard repeated accounts of culturally insensitive remarks that made the classroom feel unwelcoming, particularly for students from marginalized and/or nontraditional backgrounds.

Jim is a great professor, one of the very few professors at SI who has industry experience and knows what the industry is upto. If there is an option to take his section over any other professor, you HAVE to do it. He is also extremely understanding of students and is very accessible outside the class. Also brings in the best clients to work with.

I have taken 2 classes with Jim and they have been great! Clear instructions and a very knowledgeable professor. He also has good industry connections and has helped me a lot during my 2 years at University of Michigan.
like i guess

Is a tough grader in SI 307 and takes off high percentage of points for small reasons. This is an intro class but he makes it very difficult for someone with no figma experience. My GSI was also very unhelpful. She would be on her phone for all of our discussion sections and would not go over the assignments.

Professor Jim provides you with the essentials for UX design. He claims from the beginning he's a tough grader because he compares your work to the industry standard. Any questions asked outside of class are provided with a thorough response. I enjoy his lectures since they are jam-packed with explanations. Homework is self-motivated.

Has funny moments! Had 4 homework assignments, a midterm, a group project, and dropped the final exam for the 4th homework. Midterm was pretty difficult, but the study guide was extremely helpful. Gave 2% grade boost if you attend 20 out of 22 lectures & labs. Lectures can be dry. Lenient grading on group project, make sure to include all sections!

Professional practice as a bunch of theory. Nice lady, best academic advisor I've had so far, but her class is a bore. Most of the articles are decently interesting, but the in-class lecture is a waste of time. One or two projects are okay, but expectations were not always clear about essays. Changed the syllabus several times too, if I remember.

Let's not beat around the bush: she may be a nice person, but her teaching style is a cure for insomnia. Seems like she wants to get back to her research, quick. Yet her scholarship is mediocre at best. Disengaged. Nice smile seems to hide a lack of interest in her students. Probably a great librarian, but that doesn't make a good teacher.
Maybe she was better in her youth, but she just seemed old and feeble and not able to communicate very well. Her lectures were terribly boring. This was not an easy class.

Not sure about the class. I really liked her alot. She was helpful and nice. I know that some people don't like the fact that you choose your own readings, but I appreciated getting to choose my own boring assignments rather than being forced into on.
No Comments
No Comments

King has a teaching style that takes a while to get used to, but once you understand his approach and his expectations, youll find his class to be quite rewarding. Many of the lower reviews on this site were left mid-semester, so I think they are missing the full picture. Trust me, the ambiguities start to dissipate toward the end of the class.

A big concept of the course is "ambiguity" and I don't mean it's just one topic that's covered. The instructions, the lectures, the grading, everything is maddeningly unclear. I've never seen a professor lecture so much without ever actually saying anything. I even got my GSI to admit she had no idea what was going on despite her best efforts.

Ambiguity is NOT what I want in a class. Everything about this class is ambiguous. And the readings are dense and ramble on and on, only a small percentage of it is important. And there is SO much of it.

He lectured as if he knew everything when he made mundane pseudo-intellectual statements about how science technology & people need incentive to make a decision. He's extremely dismissive of any tough question. I expected wayyyyy better of a UM prof.

John King's style is to preach intellectually in front of the entire class, so some students may be turned off by his perpetual circle jerking. It is difficult to string his classes together to see a big picture because the topics are disconnected and he keeps everything so open ended. He also deflects tough questions if they don't fit his agenda.

This class is open-ended and vague, which many students don't like. However, it does give you a taste of what working in the real world will be like, consequently revealing the importance of critical thinking in the real world. Professor King is knowledgeable and wise, but his lectures can be a bit hard to follow or pay attention to.

John King is misunderstood by most undergrads and so is SI310. This isn't a usual class nor is he a usual professor. The course is vague, open-ended, and most students aren't used to that. That is entirely the point. Adapt and develop ways to analyze and make use of ambiguity. This class will make you a better professional, if you allow it to.

I guess he was filling in for the usual professor of this course so I have to cut him some slack, but the lectures were pretty dry and didn't really add much additional information besides what was in the readings. He just kind of stood up there and read the slides. Class was easy but not interesting or engaging.

He doesn't actually teach anything. He just tells you matter-of-fact things about people and information, and thinks that he's enlightening you. I haven't learned a single new thing from this class, except for the fact that I will avoid Prof King's time-wasting in the future.

One of the most refreshing and invigorating professors at the School of Information. One of the few professors who's made me *want* to work and attend every class and hang around afterwards. He gives an inside view into the top ranks of large organizations and their crevasses. Office hours are a treat. I wish I had another semester with him.

Prof. Markey is an excellent instructor who makes each lecture very interesting and engaging. She is also a tough grader and expects a lot from her students because she puts in a lot of time and effort in teaching them really well. She is also one of the best professors at the University of Michigan School of Information and highly recommended.
Hilarious. I understand more about the meaning of life.
